product.title: SCP Foundation Artbook | Paperback Edition | Black Journal
product.description: SCP Foundation Artbook Series by ParaBooks is based on an ambitious international web project scpwikidotcom. The Black Journal contains such famous objects as The Shy Guy, The Sculpture, Hard-to-Destroy Reptile, Able, Cain, and many other monstrous creatures and intriguing stories that will send a chill down your spine. Read detailed reports imitating real SCP Foundation documents, complemented by additional infographic materials, and illustrated by professional artists from all around the world. The book is designed with great care to look like an authentic research journal. You can even write your name on it!

reviews.reviewText: This book is a fantastic tribute to the SCP Foundation. The art is incredible, so vivid and creepy. The pages are set up to look like actual files you’re flipping through—it makes it super immersive and cool! An amazing addition to my library. I love these so much!
reviews.reviewDate: Reviewed in the United States on August 19, 2025

reviews.reviewText: Great quality and really cool artwork. Definitely recommend for fans of SCP. However it does have some spooky elements so I’d probably recommend against letting anyone under the age 17 from purchasing this book.
reviews.reviewDate: Reviewed in the United States on August 21, 2025

reviews.reviewText: The book was late on arrival. But the product was in mint condition
reviews.reviewDate: Reviewed in the United States on May 17, 2025
Reviewed in the United States on June 5, 2025

reviews.reviewText: sick book, little brother loves it
reviews.reviewDate: Reviewed in the United States on March 11, 2025

reviews.reviewText: I really enjoyed the overall quality and design and surprised at how big they were, all the little details and additional materials that were added was the cherry on top. I ordered all three, they are much cheaper than the hard cover, though still about $100 total, but I definitely got my moneys worth. I’ll definitely be willing to buy any future art books that Parabooks publish
reviews.reviewDate: Reviewed in the United States on June 6, 2023

reviews.reviewText: I can't gush about this book enough. The artwork is beautiful and the quality of the paper/book itself is outstanding for the price. My one quibble is that there are spots where a little more proofreading was needed, but these are very minor and probably not noticeable to most people. Overall, I highly recommend it.
reviews.reviewDate: Reviewed in the United States on January 24, 2024
